sourceMovie.title: The Town
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Town for the same reason it broke through in 2010: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a drama rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Ben Affleck keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The crime and drama blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Departed
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Town clicked for you, The Departed (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in crime and drama, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Town?
faq.answer: Try The Departed, Jackie Brown, A History of Violence—each shares DNA with The Town while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Town?
faq.answer: The Town (2010) is best described as Crime, Drama, Thriller. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Town worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this crime lane.
